At nearly 14 hours i officially hate this game and the reasons are below in the last paragraph. Following this i could look past most of the terrible design decisions thus initially gave it a recommended vote. There are things I appreciate, then some baffling design choices making it a frustrating and unhinged experience. For its particularly high price point I definitely cannot recommend it.  Story setup begins with a dark undertone attempting to avoid generic fantasy tropes. You're at the whim of a religious elder/priest that doesn't seem to be fond of witches. Like all terrible bosses he's too lazy to get his hands dirty and you're his abiding inquisitor. You slaughter the helpless witch likely triggering your life long quest and psychological issues that follow. Your boss isn't happy as he sees it as a mercy killing and wanted her to endure a lifetime of chronic pain and spinal issues. You're obviously paid little due to your crappy starting armor and empty gold pouch. Hopefully you find out later you're a part of a good workers union and get some spare time for therapy. First character you meet (a friend) seems morally ambiguous and unsure about the repetitively cruel tasks thats given to them. Such is life. Suddenly your character begins hearing voices. He/She is either clinically schizophrenic or there's a real life spirit haunting them.  Its an RPG with metroidvania and soulslike elements. You choose characters gender and male/female voice for the upcoming journey. There's minor choices to customize looks and choice of several different starting classes. Skill tree is nice with several branching class specific trees you can add later once hitting a specific level. Theres plenty of items to discover though the crafting system can be somewhat of a chore hunting down the needed resources. Environments and world are detailed and feel alive due to weather effects wind, rain and sunlight animation consistently avoiding static backdrops. Sound design is top quality for voice acting, effects and music. Owning surround sound speakers there's lots of little noises around you. Most sounding real like somethings going on behind me, though this might be due to long-term mental health issues. Will update mental health....I mean review once clocked in more hours.  The frustrating aspects. The controls, the bestiary, the map, generating shaders? The controls are terrible. Having the action button, run button and pickup button mapped to the same (on controller) button is infuriating. I want to run but jump on a ladder or enter a building, same with trying to pickup items i end up jumping on ladders etc.? Why cant we just auto pickup items? We will never not pickup items so just make it auto! Edit: So just experienced an entropy rift. Killed the boss and pressing the pickup item button I instead exited the rift hence losing all loot and leaving an unopened chest. Infuriating. That was the last straw. The bestiary. Why gatekeep the bestiary to an NPC that you have to travel to every time you need basic enemy stats? When you seek a particular item and want to know what enemy it drops from you have to warp to the guy gatekeeping bestiary every...single...time? The map. The guy that adds items and POI locations on your map. Why don't map icons disappear  once you collect them? You end up with a map cluttered with useless icons everywhere. The Lamp light mechanic. There are these entropy rifts throughout the game. While inside you slowly lose energy over time. You get this lamplight with a skill tree that's supposed to improve energy consumption inside etc. This mechanic is utterly uninteresting and entirely useless. The skill nodes don't do much and chasing the skill points feels entirely unrewarding because there's only several of these entropy rifts in existence. Its underbaked, wholly boring and tediously pointless. The texture shaders. Why generate nearly 4000 shaders EVERYTIME I start the game? EVERYTIME!?! It's like I'm playing on a Commodore 64.   Art and sound design are fantastic. Love the vibe and mood but these inconvenient design choices are unhinged and to some degree feel the company must hate us gamers? I can see where they put their budget as production values are decent hence the high price tag but the overall experience is worth 60% of its current price point. If you read my rant this far don't you dare buy it at full price. You'll enjoy the experience of going to your local bar, swearing at random strangers and spitting in peoples beer cups more and probably regret that experience less than buying this.

Ну игруха норм, брал за половину прайса (+-900), прошёл бы за часов 20, но вечно 3 деталька чего либо "спрятана"+ игра "давит" на тебя отсутствием надписи 100%, проходил за мага хаоса/огня через ближний бой булавой по хребту (магической булавой).  Стилек/музыка/оптимизация/визуал норм. Нравки Аватары собеседников при диалогах это пзд.... Сюжет... ну он тут есть Повтор минибоссов печалит. Сюжетные боссы.... печаль тоска. Главный враг гг - гравитация, я падал в ямы и разбивался, падал на платформы и разбивался, падал на ловушки, я пытался ухватиться за выступ без подсветки... разбивался, пытался найти тайные места.... разбивался. У меня появились крылья и двойной прыжок и я стал разбиваться в 3 раза чаще. Кста боссы хоть и пресные (кроме одного кайфарика), но их было чуть-чуть лучше пинать чем мобов. Особенные эмоции вызвала Железная дева, я как её увидел у меня весь Элден Ринг перед глазами промелькнул. Но этот босс просто мусор, я бы лучше 1к крыс в этой игре отпинал чем её, с ними хоть интереснее. (Её я зажарил лучом, в принципе как и всех скучных типов (минибоссы), хотя я и добрую часть боссов зажарил, ну не суть....)  Очень порадовал финальный босс, норм кайфарика сделали, хотя было бы круче если бы он использовал все абилки в игре во второй фазе нежели чем текущая вторая фаза, хотя она тоже ничего. Я конечно час потратил, пытаясь разной магией с телепортами и тд. Но оружие лучше булавы человечество пока ещё не придумало, и магу в лицо дать и внутренние органы бронированным рыцарям помассировать. Если булава магическая.....  Короче в танка здесь не собраться, в стеклянную пушку тоже, просто в нормиса, 70 процентов в дамаг, остальное в броню чтобы не шотали. Если по тебе нет ваншота, - враг рано или (точнее) поздно падёт.  Разрабам бы каждого босса сделать как финального и игра была бы топовой, а так 7/10, поставил бы больше, но есть минусы (мало), которые ломают кайф от плюсов (а их много).   Важная часть игры: если вы подумали что босс какой-то лох, через секунду вас шотнет, проверено экспертом. И ещё добавлю что финальный босс должен быть как Влад Дракула из V-rising,Elden, Sekiro и тд, иначе это не победа. Хотя Влад Дракула ту мач как по мне.

This game is absolutely stunningly beautiful. But did I have fun playing it? No, it quickly became a grinding chore to play. The difficulty in this game is partly (and dare I say mostly) controlled by how much damage the enemies does to you. So if you like heroic battles, you quickly find out that most enemies and bosses can either one-shot you, two-shot you or at best three-shot you. Most of the time, there is not a battle to be had as the enemies and bosses just trample over you and kill you like they step on a bug. The bosses will most of the time simply be too hard for you to beat, so you need to grind the previous area to gain levels before you can even try to beat the boss you were stuck at, only to discover that there is yet another boss shortly after, that will be too hard to beat at first, so you will need to grind the previous area some... You get the pattern by now, right? Instant death pits and traps are not nice in any game and they are used in some areas of this game to the level that you just want to crush the controller with your bare hands in frustration.  After all that said, do I recommend YOU to get this game? If it is on discount, sure why not. It is a very beautiful game after all. You might want to see it. But would you enjoy playing it, I wonder.

Pontos positivos:  1 - Árvore de habilidades atraente.  2 - Boa densidade de exploração. 3 - Possibilidade de adquirir poderes de outras classes. ~~ Pontos negativos: 1 - Lacração. Tem mulher-macho do grelo duro com queixo quadrado? 90% de chance de ter DEI na obra. De início, tentei ignorar, imaginei que podia ser só um elemento isolado. Não era. TODO HOMEM nesse jogo é um completo LIXO. Ou é um sujeito fraco como o ladrão... a história dele é interessante, vejam só: ele se acovardou, fugiu, e quem teve coragem pra explorar o calabouço foi a mulher. E sempre tem isso '' Ah eu sou um mer, mermão... queria ter a coragem dela. Ela era a verdadeira exploradora, não eu.'' Calma que trarei mais exemplos, pegue sua pipoca... Ou se o homem for forte como o ferreiro, é um betinha que tem medo de ferir as pessoas. Aí temos o idoso burro que foi enganado... temos o vampiro poderoso mas que foi escravizado por uma mulher... ai temos o vilão final que apesar de ser muito muito forte, escreveram ele pra ser detestável, óbvio...  Aí temos uma ''homenagem'' a castlevania... onde somos contratados por uma mulher pra ir atrás de um caça-vampiros chamado Richter que está morto. É sempre mulher se sobressaindo, se dando bem e é sempre homem sendo imbecil, fraco, beta, chorão, ou escroto... ''Ah era uma homenagem''. Não, não é... vocês do mandragora estão praticamente dizendo ''a nossa protagonista é mais forte e capaz que o lendário caçador de vampiros de casltevania''. Não me façam rir... castlevania tem décadas de história,.. Não existe representatividade masculina decente aqui. Aqui vemos o mesmo modus operandi de roteiristas da serie fallout. O mesmo modus operandi dos jogos feitos pela sony ocidental das últimas duas gerações. Que ia ser um jogo focado em mulheres, era meio na cara já que se trata de bruxas. Mas é sempre assim, nunca exaltam um sem rebaixar o outro. Intriguinha barata de guerra de sexos pra dividir o pilar familiar. Clássico dessa ideologia nefasta. E mesmo se vc pensar que foi só impressão, que to pegando no pé deles, não, a maluca das jóias já revelou que era lésbica e tinha uma amante. Confia, lacração não existe. Escreve lá agora '' a lactação no mundo dos games'', seu pateta tendencioso XD Isso o que tem acontecido nos últimos anos não foi meramente casos isolados, não foi a sociedade se moldando de forma orgânica e natural, mas sim produto de um plano muito bem investido por organizações milionárias com o objetivo de propagar desonestidade intelectual e lavagem-cerebral de segundo grau. Isso é um crime contra os direitos humanos, tentar moldar uma sociedade à força dessa forma.  2: Hit box. Infelizmente esse é daqueles jogos onde sofremos por limitações do software, não por culpa nossa. Eu tenho um dos joysticks mais precisos do mundo e nem ele opera milagres. Vc vai se ver desviando, saltando e mesmo no ar, mesmo com os espinhos claramente não te encostando, BUM, tome pancada. Minha opinião como game designer? Faltou frames de invencibilidade em momentos-chave. Não é sobre tornar o jogo roubado ou irrealista, mas existem momentos em que é preciso adicionar uma mudança em alguns quadros mesmo que em primeira vista não faça muito sentido. Os devs de Celeste entenderam isso. Recomendo assistirem o workshop deles.  3: Relíquias obrigatórias. A partir do momento em que o jogo me permite usar builds diferentes é algo maravilhoso. Porém, quando eu preciso carregar uma relíquia em mãos, tornando armas grandes um empecilho pro uso de magias, ai temos um problema. É 1problema porque eu fico incapaz até mesmo de COMBAR as diferentes skills. Entenda, não há tempo pra soltar a raiz e mudar pra bola de fogo já que o jogo obriga a apertar dois direcionais pra alterar por completo a build. É um botão pra mudar a arma... e outro pra mudar a hud com os outros feitiços. Isso é de uma estupidez tamanha... se supõe já que eu vou usar aquela arma especifica com aqueles feitiços, isso está denotado até mesmo no menu de equipamento, gente... foram muito tontos nesse quesito. Por que eu devo me preocupar em mudar o slot da arma E o slot de feitiço se a arma em si já bloqueia os feitiços, seja ela espadas duplas ou uma two handed? Não faz sentido... Vocês devs perderam um grande potencial que seria o de permitir magias de naturezas diferentes no mesmo set de 3 slots pra permitir combos. Como eu resolveria isso? Tornando as relíquias pedrinhas que flutuam ou ficam em algum encaixe na roupa da personagem, atuando de forma PASSIVA, não como algo que literalmente me faz escolher entre soltar bolas de fogo e ficar com uma espadinha media ou usar uma marreta de guerra e não usar feitiço algum...  4: Repetição. Em menos de 5 horas de gameplay lutei contra 3 chefes ratos gigantes. Galera, calma lá... o que é isso? Não deram nem tempo direito. O pior nem é isso, é que depois vemos mais e mais e mais variantes de chefes de novo. Acho que de gigantes existem uns 7 pelo jogo... rato tem uns 9... cachorrão das trevas? Uns 7 também. É tudo muito repetitivo. E não pensem que não reparei: existem inimigos que são as mesmas variantes de outros, mas com uma carinha diferente. Um é um lobisomem só que invisível dessa vez... o outro é um ''morto-vivo assassino'' que funciona exatamente como o ladrão habilidoso... isso tudo soa como preguiça, como safadeza... Deixem seu jogo menor, nem que dure menos horas, mas não cometam essas atrocidades, por favor. Mesmo boa porção dos feitiços também são variáveis apenas com outro visual. Era melhor fazer menos feitiços do que repetí-los e achar que ninguém ia perceber.  5: Falta de direção. Haverá momentos em que você não saberá como prosseguir. Você terá que entrar em uma das fendas entrópicas e torcer pra achar o portal que lidere até seu destino pra outra porção do mapa. E isso não está dito em lugar algum.   6: Não há reestoque nos vendedores. Existem itens ilimitados e itens limitados. As vezes vai faltar um couro, uma linha, um ferro pra dar upgrade neles. Ai você vai é ter que se virar e ir procurando pelo mapa mesmo já que acabou o estoque dessas coisas. Ninguém no mundo tem mais ferro, gente! Vamos voltar a fazer lanças com madeira e pedra, uga buga! Mas ok, isso é o que menos irrita. Andando o suficiente dá pra coletar tudo, porém entendam uma coisa: eu sei que vocês queriam equilibrar o jogo tomando essa decisão, só que as coisas já estão equilibradas a partir do momento em que eu gasto meu dinheiro com esses itens. A diferença aqui é que eu gastaria meu tempo farmando gold dos ladrões, onde eu sei que tem de sobra, em vez de sair pelo mapa correndo atrás de cada ingrediente desgraçado em localizações diferentes. Ao tomar essa decisão, vocês REMOVEM o poder de liberdade do jogador. Às vezes, alguém quer guardar dinheiro pra comprar uma arma poderosa. Deixe que essas pessoas vão explorar pra manter seus bolsos cheios. Às vezes, quem está satisfeito com seus equipamentos, apenas quer a facilidade, mesmo que isso signifique torrar toda a sua grana. Haja o que houver... a escolha PRECISA existir. ISSO é game design inteligente.  Conclusão: É um jogo para homens, mas sobre cuié. É um metroidvania com elementos de soulslike. Deus do céu, como eu abomino essa junção de palavras ''elementos de ''. É aí que vc sabe que vem um jogo bomba. Quem imaginava em sã consciência anos atrás colocar barra de stamina em um metroidvania? A konami e a nintendo não eram nem loucas de implementar isso. O jogo permite ir até o lv600 mais ou menos, sei lá, ''falaro''. Dá pra zerar duas vezes SE você quiser testar build. Até as escolhas de diálogos são pura ilusão, bem estilo jogos de Persona. Existem acho que três diálogos específicos onde o que você escolher pode acarretar em uma luta, mas só. Se não tivesse DEI, aumentaria minha nota, recomendaria, com toda certeza. Eu sei o que essa ideologia faz com as pessoas e não posso me apaziguar quando vejo isso sendo propagado. Sempre será não para qualquer um que coloque essas porcarias em seus jogos. ~~ NOTA: 6.0

[h1]A very enjoyable Soulslike-Metroidvania that deserves more attention.[/h1]  I went into [b]Mandragora: Whispers of the Witch Tree[/b] expecting a brutal final challenge after seeing many players talk about how difficult the last boss could be.  In reality, the fight is much more manageable than people make it seem. I completed the game using what many players consider one of the hardest approaches for that fight: [b]a melee two-handed greatsword build[/b], mixed with [b]fire magic[/b], [b]bleed[/b], and some [b]life-steal (“vampirism”) mechanics[/b]. Most discussions online recommend heavy magic builds for the final encounter, but even with a slower melee playstyle the fight felt fair and very doable.  The game itself starts a bit slowly. The early hours can feel slightly drawn out while the systems, world, and pacing settle into place. But once everything clicks, the experience becomes genuinely enjoyable.  The combat feels satisfying, the build variety is interesting, and the exploration slowly opens up in a way that rewards patience. Most importantly, the game finishes strong. The late-game encounters and progression make the earlier slow pacing feel worthwhile.  [b]Mandragora may not be the hardest game in the genre, but it is definitely a fun and memorable one.[/b] If you enjoy Soulslike combat mixed with Metroidvania exploration, it’s absolutely worth your time.

No suelo hacer reseñas en ningún sitio pero creo que merece la pena cuando la ocasión lo merece. Porque Mandragora: Whispers of the Witch Tree es un juego hecho desde el cariño.   Cuando jugué la demo hace ya tiempo me atrapó por completo: su atmósfera, la banda sonora, el arte, el doblaje, su mundo... Es sorprendente y triste a la vez que un estudio tan pequeño y tan poco conocido (que yo sepa éste es su primer y único juego) tenga más calidad que otros mucho más grandes con muchísimo más presupuesto.   El mundo tiene "vida". Hablas con bastantes personajes y cada uno tiene sus intereses y su personalidad. No es un mundo "vacío" o "muerto" como en otros juegos similares como Blasphemous o Hollow Knight, que también me parecieron obras maestras y creo que Mandragora no tiene absolutamente nada que envidiarles.   Jugablemente además creo que es una gozada. He visto alguna queja por ahí en lo referente al movimiento. En mi opinión no se siente tosco para nada, al fin y al cabo creo que es justo lo que pretende. Estás controlando a un humano. Aquí no esquivas casi teletransportándote ni haces saltos kilométricos. El mapa es enorme, me ha sorprendido lo grande que es y lo bien que funciona. El viaje rápido creo que es otro acierto y hace que explorar sea menos tedioso que otros metroidvanias. Me parece genial también que haya una gestión de inventario y loot algo similar a Path of Exile y Diablo (estoy seguro de que se han inspirado en ellos), creo que es un acierto enorme que encaja a la perfección con este título. Por otro lado, la cantidad y variedad de enemigos es brutal. Más de 100 diferentes!  Hay muchísimos objetos, armaduras, ropa, armas, ingredientes... es una locura, pero no se siente forzado, y en cierto modo el juego te invita a que adquieras mejor equipo a través del crafteo con los personajes de tu "base" y que de vez en cuando te pases por allí para mejorar y fabricar.   Hacía tiempo que no me divertía tanto y me sentía tan atrapado por un universo. Mi enhorabuena a los chicos de Primal Game Studio. Ojalá tengamos una segunda parte en algún momento.   Totalmente recomendado. Lo volvería a comprar sin dudarlo.

простой сюжет, простой геймплей, игра не особо выдающаяся, но с приятной картинкой и в нее приятно играть, удачное сочетание соулса и метроидвании. Вот прокачка перса прикольная. Я хорошо провел время, короче.

Plusy: + Piękna oprawa wizualna - projekty lokacji od podupadających miast nękanych przez bandytów i monstra przez mroczne lasy pełne krypt i katakumb po mroźne, skaliste równiny najeżone pułapkami są szczegółowo dopracowane, zawierając mnóstwo detali dla wprawnego oka; + Gęsty, opresyjny klimat - styl dark fantasy w wymiarze 2,5D urzeka, budując atmosferę tajemniczości i nadciągającego zagrożenia wiszącego nad protagonistą oraz Faelduum niczym Miecz Damoklesa; + Swoboda personalizacji bohatera - szereg klas oraz rozbudowane drzewka umiejętności prowadzą do powstania różnych, momentami niespotykanych buildów. Opcja łączenia zdolności (po osiągnięciu odpowiedniego poziomu) dwóch klas wprowadza element elastyczności w prowadzonym stylu walki; + Walka souls-lite trąci - satysfakcjonująca, sprawiedliwa choć mało wymagająca. Zarządzanie wytrzymałością i mocą magiczną, uniki w tempo ciosów (timing), nauka schematów ataków przeciwników. Różnorodność humanoidalnych i niehumanoidalnych wrogów. Kilka przyjemnych, dobrze zaprojektowanych i zrealizowanych starć z bossami. Odczuwalny ciężar broni. Impact hit po zadaniu obrażeń skutkujący przełamaniem równowagi; + Alternatywny wymiar - latarnia wiedźmy, jeden z przedmiotów fabularnych, umożliwia otwieranie portali do krainy chaosu (Entropii) zawierającej nowe wyzwania w postaci zagadek środowiskowych oraz walk z bossami. Eksploracja tego miejsca niesie jednak ze sobą pewne koszta, możliwe do zminimalizowania za pomocą korzeni mandragory.  Minusy: - Powierzchowność systemów - elementy takie jak crafting, zadania poboczne czy drzewko umiejętności są zrealizowane poprawnie acz bez polotu; - Blada narracja - historia, w wyniku braku emocjonalnego zaangażowania, często schodzi na dalszy plan ustępując miejsca eksploracji Fealduum. Na swojej ścieżce spotkamy postacie niezależne, które zdecydują się dołączyć do naszego zespołu oferując w zamian usługi handlu czy craftingu. Nie wnoszą jednak one (poza drobnymi wyjątkami) nic do opowieści będąc jedynie jej dodatkiem na uboczu niż pełnoprawnym uczestnikiem; - Ekonomia obozu - Ceny oferowannych usług i towarów są zdecydowanie zbyt wysokie prowadząc do szybkiej utraty złotych monet nawet po zwiększeniu poziomów stanowisk gdzie każdy poziom niesie ze sobą jakiegoś rodzaju zniżkę. Dodatkowo, zapasy (żywność, rośliny, strzały itp.) u kupców nie uzupełniają się przez co dochodzi do absurdalnych sytuacji gdzie gra wymusza na Tobie zakup mikstur many ponieważ brakuje Ci jednego ze składników niezbędnego do procesu warzenia, który rośnie w jednym jedynym miejscu w niewystarczającej ilości zaś obozowy alchemik nie trudzi się by odnowić czy zwiększyć jego ilość na stanie; - Zręcznościowy niewypał - brak możliwości oddania precyzyjnych skoków z powodu nieresponywnego, nieprecyzyjnego sterowania w sekcjach platformowych skutkuje upadkiem z wysokości. W zależności od odległości od ziemi jest on obarczony dotkliwą karą utraty dużej ilości punktów zdrowia czy natychmiastową śmiercią protagonisty; - Powtarzalność mini-bossów - brak pomysłów, czasu bądź brak środków finansowych na odpowiednie zagospodarowanie przestrzeni interesującymi mini-bossami spowodował wysoki recyking assetów. Z danym wariantem mini-bossa jesteśmy w stanie walczyć nawet do 7 razy.  Ocena: 8/10

Core combat gameplay is extremely boring and feedback / impact / audio design makes it feel terrible to attack enemies.   Powers and abilities are incredibly, extremely uninspired and boring. These devs obviously subscribe to the "no it can't look / feel too cool, think about the lore!" philosophy that is seeing western games (among other reasons) get left in the dust by games from the east.  Massive "talent tree" that really just boils down to a convoluted way to allocate stats and a few incredibly meh perks.   Weapon and armor design are garish, ugly and aren't satisfying to use.   Nice visuals and production value in that regard.  Terrible and I mean terrible music. They could've used AI and had music that was 10x better.  Story is...slow, boring and leaves you with the question "oh, right, stuff is supposed to be happening" because the pacing of the world and game progression is so off.  Crafting and customization is a joke. Like, they got a first year college student to come up with it.   TLDR - Great visuals and voice acting, everything else is pretty bad to terrible. Gameplay design is the culprit for the most part.

10/10 This game is a masterpiece in flawless execution.  There is literally nothing negative to say about this game.  It is a metroidvania in the full sense that you will explore and find upgrades that let you progress further into the game while having moderate platforming sessions using very high quality controls.  You will also get a fully engaging story with all the rpg mechanics you've come to expect.  Picture Elden Ring but without the unfair mechanics of the game "cheating"  Controls - Very easy and accurate.  Only 1 jump height but it works in this game.  Jumping challenges - Limited - Your grandparents could complete the jumps with ease.  Combat - Simple but quite varied.  Many melee and spell options.  Bosses - Easy enough to learn, tough enough to make you die, fair enough to continue through the game without anger.  Boss Run Backs - Minimal, you'll be back in the fight in no time.  Map - One of the greatest thought-out map designs.  Especially when you get the map fragments and then the map shows which doors are locked, which floors you can break through, where the plants and ore are located, etc..  The multi layer color coding per section along with the notification of (100% area).   Voice Acting - The entire game is voice acted and it's done well.  I adore Yrsa's voice (same VA from Karliah in Skyrim).  Artistic Design - To me, this is where this game leaves me speechless.   The artwork, animations, attention to all details, the soundtrack, the ambient noise and effect sounds..... The entire world and level design is done so well it should be in art gallery!  Story - Great!  Not very complex but it suited the narrative and was enjoyable throughout.  Developer choices - There is no level cap!  There is also multiple new-game+ modes.  You can take that same character and go get every skill node if you want to invent the time.  The new-game+ modes also have + items to match the new difficulty.  Multiple Endings too!  I completed the game in about 40-45 hours getting every achievement but 2,  (The grindy kill count achievements), an I instantly started new game +1.  New Game+ - You bring you character, your full inventory, your NPC recipes and NPC training tiers, your level and skill points.   You DO NOT bring the movement/traversal skills you unlock during the main quest, you will have to learn them as you do your new-game+ quests.

Ein gutes Spiel, cozy Soulslike, würde ich sagen, da es doch allerhand Quality of Life bietet, auf die meisten genretypischen Ärgernisse (wie z. B. lange Runbacks) verzichtet, auch ziemlich leicht anfängt und sich moderat mit einigen Schwierigkeitsspitzen entwickelt.  Das Kampf-Gameplay ist grundlegend simpel gehalten: Es gibt den Schildblock (der a la Dark Souls Ausdauer kostet) oder eine Ausweichrolle, ansonsten normale und Spezialangriffe, die Adrenalin kosten. Oder man nutzt für Mana Zauber (ich habe aber einen reinen Nahkämpfer gespielt). Daran ist wenig neu, aber es ist absolut funktional und hinsichtlich des notwendigen Timings recht verzeihend. Auch mehrere Gegner (was häufig vorkommt) sind gut bewältigbar. Die Bosse sind überwiegend interessant gestaltet, einige durchaus etwas fordernd (was dann einen guten Flow erzeugte), Mini-Bosse wiederholen sich für meinen Geschmack etwas zu oft und sind auch zu einfach. Man sollte es mögen, Patterns zu lernen, aber im Grunde klappt das hier einigermaßen intuitiv. Da es umfangreiche Statistiken gibt, weiß ich, dass ich für die schwierigsten fünf Bosse 6-9 Versuche brauchte, für den letzten Endboss 20. 30mal hat mich die "Umgebung" getötet, d. h., ich bin in den Abgrund gefallen. Die Plattforming-Passagen sind aber selten anspruchsvoll. Später spielen Bewegungsoptionen (Doppelsprung, Gleiten) jedoch auch in Bosskämpfen eine Rolle, diese Optionen schaltet man aber erst im letzten Drittel frei, was ein bisschen Schade ist. Zum Aufleveln gibt es Skill Trees bzw. "Sphärobretter" (also mit Felder, die Attributsbonus gewähren neben solchen mit Fähigkeiten), wobei man zunächst eine Klasse wählt, darunter auch verschiedene Zauberer zur Wahl hat, und ab Stufe 25 Multclassen kann, was den Tüftlern und Optimierern sicher zusagt. Um das Spiel durchzuspielen, ist es aber nicht nötig, sich besonders tief damit auseinanderzusetzen.   Die Erkundung der Welt macht Spaß, bietet manche Abzweigungen zum Erkunden, ist aber an sich nicht besonders frei. Und Schätze lohnen sich, da man "Diagramme" findet, im Grunde Pläne für Ausrüstung, Support Items, Gebietskarten. Die wiederum kann man zu NPCs in den Hub bringen, welche dadurch sowie durch das Herstellen der Gegenstände auch aufleveln. Da gibt es dann den Schmied, die Alchemistin etc. Dass man für manche Gegenstände erst einiges Sinnloses herstellen muss, nur um das Mindestlevel zu erreichen, nervte zwar ein wenig, kam aber auch nicht so häufig vor. Meist hat sich die Progression recht organisch ergeben. Das Waffenarsenal reicht von Dolchen, über Ein- und Zweihandschwerter bzw. Streitkolben bis zum Zauberkram. Die Werte sind transparent (z. B. sogar die Angriffsgeschwindigkeit) und man kann Runen zur Spezialisierung einsetzen (auch auswechseln, die Ressourcen reichen locker). Kein übermäßig breites Angebot, aber die Waffentypen spielen sich unterschiedlich und funktionieren auch auf ihre Weise, soweit ich das ausprobiert habe (zu Magie kann ich nichts sagen).  Optisch gibt es nichts auszusetzen, die 2,5D-Elemente stören nur ganz vereinzelt: Wenn man mal im Eifer des Gefechts nicht ganz erkennt, dass eine Plattform schon zu Ende ist. Die Sicht auf die Spielfigur wird nie verdeckt. Kulisse und Soundtrack sind eigentlich immer atmosphärisch, Dark Fantasy typisch. Wobei mir bei den Tracks die bisweilen progressive Songstruktur aufgefallen ist, dass Melodien auch mal stärker variieren und ein Thema verarbeiten. Das ist also nicht der Standard-"epische Chorus". Auch die Gegnervielfalt ist in Ordnung. Wenn es Gegnervarianten gibt, dann verfügen die stets über andere Fähigkeiten oder Eigenschaften: Das eine fliegende Auge strahlt in Frequenz eine Schadensnova aus, das nächste hinterlässt eine Giftspur, das dritte schießt einen Strahl bei Blickkontakt. Die verschiedenen Hexenmeister-Typen haben unterschiedliche Zauber auf Lager. So etwas eben. Die Story ist solide, mehr aber auch nicht. Einige Elemente - z. B. die Inquisitoren-Kollegen, die zu Beginn eingeführt werden - bleiben lange vernachlässigt. Im späteren Mittelteil hat das Pacing einige Längen. Am Ende zieht es dann aber an und holte mich auch für einen zweiten Teil/DLC ab (was klar angedeutet wird).  Alles in allem - wie eingangs gesagt - gut: 7,5 / 10. Wer ein klassisches 2D-Soulslike mit Metroidvania-Elementen sucht, das sich auch sehen sowie hören lassen kann und einigen Spielumfang mit wenig Längen sucht; wer Dark Fantasy mag und über das eine oder andere Klischee hinwegsehen kann; wer eher sanft gefordert werden möchte, der hat hier bestimmt einen Fund gemacht.  Ich hatte es im Sale für ca. 23,- € erstanden, der Vollpreis (39,99,- €) wäre mir dann doch etwas happig für das Gebotene.

Oh yeah, this was a really great Metroidvania with some weird quircks here and there. The game looks and sounds great and the music is ...well..there. But nothing memorable. Gameplay can change a lot between builds you can do so I can't really say anything about that. Spells felt fine to utilize tho.  Most of the playthrough I stuck with my initial class by default until in the lateish game I got stuck to a ..certain.. boss for quite a while. The current build was just so slow to cast it's spells that I couldn't manage to fight it at all. (Died like 60-70 times..) So I had to change my build to a melee one just for that one. Otherwise I don't really have anything bad to say about the game. Bosses were mostly fun to learn and fight. A lot of same bosses were reused tho so there's one flaw I can say.. I wasn't lost in any part of the game, secrets were clear and well indicated. There was complete % on the map that you could follow to find more secrets too.  I recommend this game overall. It's not that expensive and you can even get it on sale quite often. Fans of more "dark soul-ish" metroidvania players will like this one.

The game is close to being incredible, but every time it disappoints.  I've barely been here 10 hours and I've already noticed major problems (apparently, the end is even worse...).  Too many traps or other things that kill you in one shot, it's just back and forth for nothing and it doesn't add difficulty, just die and retry, too many traps make the game frustrating.  All bosses have summons to make the fight harder, otherwise they would be too easy.  I've already faced the same rat boss four times and several other identical bosses with the same pattern...  I have the best gear possible for my level, well optimised, and my skills are well balanced, yet I feel like everything kills me in 2 or 3 hits.  Monsters jumping out of the screen and inflicting enormous damage on you for free...  Not enough equipment. I still have my starting weapon because I don't have anything better, even though I've completed 100% of all the previous areas.  Some quests are too high level in low level story areas, so we struggle for nothing because the boss is 30 levels higher than us.  The demo is the best part of the game.

Эта игра свалилась на меня неожиданно, но зашла на все 100 %.  Получил её в подарок, до этого даже не слышал про такую. Настрой был скептический: «Ну, ещё одна метроидвания, чего там…» Но игра моментально зацеила - первые 50 часов пролетели быстро. Боссы, секретные локации, боевая система - всё это цепляет и не отпускает. Правда, боевая система сначала кажется какой‑то… деревянной, но это временно.  Что реально зашло Визуал. Картинка радует глаз в 99 % случаев: закаты, подземелья, пылающие города, монстры, боссы, эффекты магии и ударов - всё выглядит сочно и атмосферно. Никаких визуальных просадок, глаз не устаёт даже после десятого входа в игру.  Саунд - просто огонь. Музыка не надоедает, не режет уши, а наоборот - задаёт "вайб" на каждой локации. Звуковые эффекты монстров - отдельный вид искусства: рыки, крики, визги и хрипы звучат так, как и должны. Сразу понимаешь, кто перед тобой - и хочется либо завалить, либо сбежать (зависит от уровня).  Прокачка - чувствуется. Сначала кажется, что уровни особо ничего не дают, но после очередных 10‑ти уровней персонаж начинает нарезать рядовых мобов быстрее, а боссы уже не убивают так быстро. В НГ+ это ощущается ещё ярче - можно перебросить очки в другую ветку и раскрывать персонажа по‑новому.   Бестиарий - не скучаем. Монстров много, они разные, и убивать их не надоедает. Боссы - отдельная тема: некоторые реально заставляют попотеть, а их мувсеты не повторяются (кроме родственных типов - крысы, демоны и т. д.). Не так сложно, чтобы выбеситься, но достаточно, чтобы подумать над тактикой.  Реиграбельность - да, сэр! Прошёл на НГ+, потом на НГ+1, потом на НГ+2 - и всё ещё цепляет. Боссы становятся жёстче, ваншотят уже не с одного удара, а с 0,75 удара, и это заставляет менять билд, пробовать новые комбинации способностей и искать слабые места.  Спорные моменты Боевая система - зависит от класса. Я играл за кинжальщика‑ядовика (местный аналог роги из WoW), и мне в целом зашло. Но есть нюансы: Первая треть игры: способностей мало, комбо не клеятся, ощущения «вау» нет. Вторая треть: появляются ключевые скиллы - и процесс ускоряется, становится веселее. Конец игры: руки наконец‑то растут откуда надо, комбо получаются, боссы падают быстрее. Проблема в том, что за другие классы играть не так драйвово - баланс явно перекошен в пользу определённых билдов. Способности - мусор vs имба. Есть скиллы, которые читаешь и сразу понимаешь: никогда не возьму. И даже улучшения не спасают - они такие же бесполезные.   Сайд‑квесты - награда не стоит свеч. Да, есть парочка забавных и интересных, но лут за них такой, что можно сразу выкинуть в мусорку. Кто‑то скажет: «Ну зато лор же!» А я отвечу: «Ха!» - про лор ниже.  Где игра реально подвела Сюжет - проходной. Стандартный, заезженный, видели уже тысячу раз. В 2026 году такое как‑то… неуместно. Понимаю, что метроидвания — это не про историю, но тут сюжет настолько серый, что даже не запоминается.  Крафт - 50/50. Для моего класса 90 % крафтовых шмоток стали бесполезны уже с первой трети игры. Зато расходники (банки, бижа, еда) сделаны на уровне. Но вот беда: ингредиенты для крафта постоянно заканчиваются, и ты либо бегаешь их фармить (раз в Н-количество часов), либо играешь без баффов - а это уже минус к эффективности.  Левел‑дизайн - боль и страдания. В некоторых местах я умирал от внезапных падений раз пять подряд, и дело точно было не во мне (ну, почти). Иногда кажется, что дизайнер уровней был не в себе, когда создавал эти участки. Плохой левел‑дизайн убил больше нервных клеток, чем боссы. До сих пор помню, как потерял три уровня, упав в пропасть, а потом возродился, побежал за душами и умер точно так же. Классика.  Расположение костров - мем уровня «дарксоулз момент». Чтобы добраться до босса для реванша, нужно пробиться через орду мобов и перепрыгнуть кучу ям. К моменту подхода к арене ты уже вымотан, и босс тебя просто добивает взглядом.  Финальный вердикт Игра хорошая, но не идеальная. Есть моменты, которые хочется пофиксить, но в целом она стоит того, чтобы в неё поиграть.  Купил бы я её сам? Нет. Хороша ли она в качестве подарка? Абсолютно!  Если вам нравится:  метроидвании с открытым миром, прокачка и создание уникальных билдов, боссами, с которыми придётся повозиться, но с ума не сведут, исследование локаций в поисках секретов, - эта игра вам понравится.

Overall a really solid souls-like metroidvania. I used an ultrawide mod to play it at 3440 x 1440 and it worked almost perfectly so it makes me wonder why the devs didn't put ultrawide support in on their own. I got this game on sale and it was very worth it. There are many build options for your character and the combat is pretty satisfying. Like people have said, some bosses get re-used a lot but FROM does the exact same thing in their games and people still love them so it is what it is. I didn't mind that personally. I really love the art in this game too it's gorgeous and I hope these devs make another game like this or a sequel.

[h3]Прикольная метроидвания в духе мрачных сказок братьев Гримм. Наверно, первая метроидвания, которая мне реально понравилась после Hollow Knight. Геймплей несколько репетативный, но визуал и исследование мира доставили массу удовольствия. Поэтому могу смело рекомендовать к покупке![/h3]  [u][b]За здравие![/b][/u]🍻 ✅ Качественный визуал, арт и анимации. Игра выглядит дорогобогато. ✅ История зашла, особенно по началу. Как я понял, с нарративом помогал Брайан Митсода, это создатель Bloodlines. Поэтому следил за повествованием не без интереса, диалоги не скучные, местами даже цепляют. Атмосфера мрачной сказки выдержана хорошо. ✅ Боевка доставляет. Есть возможность комбинировать классы, но глубины не хватает, я в основном проходил за мага с одним набором скиллов от начала и до конца. В целом работает, но хотелось бы чуть больше вариативности. ✅ Что мне действительно понравилось, так это платформинг, изучение мира и всякие секретики. Вот это сделано реально круто. Без перегибов и занудных участков🥰  [u][b]За упокой[/b][/u]🍺 ❌ Как обычно, Unreal и все, что с ним связано🤬 Со временем игра начинает просто плохо работать, видимо из-за утечек памяти. Помогает только рестарт. ❌ Чем глубже в лес, тем толще партизаны, сюжет под конец сдувается. ❌ Геймплей со временем начинает ощущаться более однообразным😔

Mandragora absolutely blew me away.  Visually, this is one of the most stunning games I’ve played in the genre. Every environment feels handcrafted with care and atmosphere. It’s the kind of game where you genuinely stop just to admire the scenery. Experiencing the world is a pleasure in itself.  The map design is excellent - clear, functional, and actually helpful. Exploration feels natural and rewarding, never confusing for the wrong reasons. There’s a constant sense of discovery, adventure, and forward momentum.  What really surprised me was the depth of customization. The variety of classes, skills, and equipment allows for very different playstyles — and the ability to multiclass opens even more possibilities. Whether you prefer brute force, magic, mobility, or hybrid builds, the game supports it.  Another huge plus: accessibility options. You can adjust the experience to match your skill level, which makes the game welcoming without removing the challenge for those who want it.  After reading some Steam reviews, I honestly feel this game is underrated and unfairly judged. It deserves far more attention than it’s getting. For anyone who loves exploration, build diversity, and beautifully crafted worlds - this is absolutely worth your time.

Мир пал под гнётом Неведомого, и ты — лишь искра, блуждающая в густом тумане Фельдума. Древо-Ведьма шепчет твоё имя, а за пределами безопасных стен Града Энтропия пожирает саму суть реальности. Приготовь свой клинок и зажги фонарь, путник. Здесь надежда умирает последней, но умирает обязательно. Mandragora — Песнь стали в мире, забытом богами Тьма в 2.5D (Атмосфера и Мир) Забудь о ярких красках. Перед тобой мрачное полотно в стиле сайд-скроллера, где каждый задний план дышит угрозой, а каждый корень тянется к твоему горлу. Это мир «тёмного фэнтези», где готика встречается с безумием Лавкрафта. Ты пройдешь через выжженные земли и проклятые леса, чтобы понять: чудовища снаружи — лишь отражение того, что скрыто внутри нас. Цена мастерства (Геймплей и Ролевая система) Ты не просто машешь мечом. Выбирай свой путь: станешь ли ты сокрушительным Вангардом или искусным мастером стихий? Древо талантов здесь ветвится, как корни самой Ведьмы. Сложные бои в стиле Souls-like не прощают суеты. Один неверный кувырок, одна затянутая атака — и Энтропия заберет твою душу. Учись парировать, сочетай навыки и помни: твоя сила растет на крови павших врагов. Мастерская в пустоте (Крафт и Караван) Ты не одинок в этом походе. Собирай ремесленников, расширяй свой лагерь и создавай снаряжение, способное выдержать удар бога. Каждый найденный чертеж, каждый кусок редкой руды — это твой шанс прожить на минуту дольше. Твой караван — это последний оплот человечества в океане хаоса. Шепот судьбы (Сюжет) Моральные дилеммы здесь бьют больнее, чем боссы. Кому ты доверишься? Кого принесешь в жертву ради высшего блага? Древо-Ведьма видит все твои решения, и финал твоей истории будет написан чернилами твоих поступков. Вердикт Mandragora — это испытание для тех, кто любит боль, эстетику упадка и глубокую прокачку. Это красиво, это жестоко и это затягивает в свои сети, как зов сирены.

Очень жалею, что не рефанднул в первые 2 часа. Но за это время данный "шедевр" не успевает себя раскрыть. Плохо сделано всё, кроме арт дизайна. 1) Боёвка. "Давайте сделаем каслванию, но шоб как соуслайк, с стаминой. УЭЭЭЭЭ!" В бою персонаж себя ведёт как кусок желе, причём не самого свежего, анимации - длинющие, хитбоксы - не кривые даже, они просто "типа есть". Комбо как таковых не обнаружено, играл за мага, мб, у "воинов" работает иначе. Арены с боссами - отвратительные, рич боссов придумал явно фанат Бабадзаки, но, который уж слишком сильно любит боллбастинг стальными шпильками от домин ростом с леди Диметреску.  2) Платформинг - фрустрирует донельзя, персонаж практически не управляем, залезать на уступы на геймпаде сущий ад, сдвинул курок на милиметр - всё, прыгаем вниз.  3) Ловушки. Я сначала думал, что для обхода маятников нужен особый навык какой-то внутриигровой, потом понял, что там нужно ловить пиксели, а, напомню, двигается персонаж как несвежая желешка. И атаки врагов кувырком можно задоджить, а вот маятник - нет, дружок, лови пиксели. 4) Торговцы - "Здравствуй, башня Орокин! Здравствуй, милый нитаин! Каждодневный душный фарм, за тебяяяя я жизнь отдааам!" © Кто понял - тот поймёт, кто не понял - чтобы получить возможность крафтить и покупать что-то хуже тир 0 предметов придётся гриндить как в Рагнарок Онлайн, в сингл, мать его, плеерной метроидвании. 5) Про боссов в боёвке уже пожаловался, но они, во-первых, скучные, во-вторых, половина из них спаунит постоянно адов, которые не делают бой интереснее, а только и без того мелкую арену превращают в минное поле. Бой с Петером это вообще какой-то анекдот категории "Г". Хоть, концептуально, он мог бы быть очень хорош.  6) Прокачка - "Уэээээ! Давайте возьмём ну эта, древо талантов из ПОЕ, только ни хрена ещё не будем пояснять, что за что отвечает, уээээээ!" У меня 3 всевышних образований и времени копаться в недоступной фекстралайф нет, чтобы билд не запороть, потому что гипотенуза и котангенс.   В итоге получается нечто, которое собрано из кусков кастлы, солсов, ММО гриндилок и "РПГ", только этот гомункул настолько уродлив, что полюбить его могут либо законченные мазохисты-извращенцы, либо те, кто с выше указанными жанрами не был знаком никогда, либо кто-то, кто вылез из бункера, в котором сидел с 2007 и решил теперь приобщиться к играм.

Хороший платформер с интересной боевкой. Не заметил, как пролетели 40 часов. Хорошо спроектированная карта, постоянно открываешь какие-то новые локации, пути, возможности, не можешь остановиться и сказать: "Ну на текущий момент сделал все, что можно".  Отличная графика, приятная рисовка, только для 2д платформера жрет слишком много ресурсов — на 5090, в 4к и ограничении 180 кадров видеокарта работает под 100%.  Хороший интерфейс с подсказками, словарем, бестиарием, коллекциями и картой. Практически все идеально, единственное не написано где и какой моб спавнится, даже локации не указано, из-за этого пришлось искать информацию в интернете, чтобы выполнить достижение на бестиарий.  Очень много различных механик, типов урона и эффектов. Сделаны они качественно, но к сожалению игра вообще не раскрывает этот аспект. За все прохождение я вообще ни разу об этом не задумался. За все прохождение я сменил всего две пары клинков и два комплекта кожаной брони. Практически не использовал всякие элексиры и еду, т.к. супер душно собирать ресурсы для них, а толку не так много. Использовал всего два вида рун для оружия и брони, под свой билд, остальные вообще ни разу не пригодились.  Очень много навыков, но я качал просто зеленую ветку по мере получения уровней, особо не задумываясь — все вкладывал в урон. Игра за это никак не наказывает. В принципе она не сильно сложная, всего пара боссов вызвали какие-то сложности и то, пока не выучил их скудный мувсет. И это практически без использования каких-либо фласок и масел.  Сюжет не сказать, что сильно плох, но очень примитивен. Все твисты читаются с нулевой. Возможно это сделано специально, но в том числе и из-за этого вся история достаточно скучная.  В общем игра не плохая, но не раскрыла весь своей потенциал. Было прикольно побегать 40 часов и повыполнять все ачивки.

man...   I really wanted to love this one. the story is starting off quite intriguing and the game overall feels quite atmospheric. but there were a few things that made me hesitate as I kept playing.   first off, the character model you play feels extremely low effort, and the character animations make it even worse. on top of that, the voice for the main character didn't really match the rest of the game's vibes, it pulled me out of the immersion of the game a number of times. I was willing to look past that because the rest of the world and art direction was definitely captivating.   but then after levelling a few times and looking through the skill tree... I was hoping to see some expansion on the combat aside from just melee smacking and dodging, but it looks like there are no additional skills aside from the little crappy fireball you get at the start (at least for my class in particular). the fireball could have at least been a cool animation or something to be a little bit interesting, but it felt more like an afterthought. I could get see the combat getting quite mundane and repetitive as you spend more time with the game. it leaves a lot to be desired tbh. because of that, I'm worried about going past the 2 hour mark on this game as I feel like I've already seen most of what the game will potentially offer me already.  I think that if they went fixed these things for me it could be a game I see myself sinking hours into... but as of right now, I'm a bit unsure. it feels like it has the foundation of what could make it good, but it lacks in execution and life in a lot of aspects.

7/10  I picked this up after finishing Grime while waiting for Grime II. It's a lot of fun and has a good amount of content. You've got a handful of classes to start with, each mostly built around a unique on-hit effect like entangle, weakness, poison, etc. You unlock NPCs to join you along way who can be upgraded to offer better gear/items, which can be crafted from materials you either gather from the environment or farm from enemies. Combat is smooth and follows the souls-like format of stamina management and being punished for greed. There is a great big passive skill tree for each character, and you can branch over to other characters' trees if you like. The tree is pretty standard though, so there isn't really much build crafting or variety. It's just a solid metroidvania that is engaging and fun until the end.  Quick notes: - You can respec very cheaply. This is unlocked pretty early in the game. - Weapons have the A-E scaling for stats. - Weapons and gear cannot be upgraded, but they can be slotted with a single rune for bonuses. - Runes can be destroyed and replaced with better ones. - Armor has weight, so you have to invest in some stats to avoid fat rolling. - Mini bosses are fun, and main bosses are unique. Some of them are quite difficult. - Traversal ability unlocks are spaced out a bit too much for my liking. - Everything is voice acted well. - Some classes are clearly better than others. - The map is huge. - Fast travel is unlocked very early. - Casual completion was about 30h, that includes discovering the whole map and backtracking for secrets. - You can choose from NG+0 - NG+4, which is accessible from the pause menu at any time. - NG+ offers a few new pieces of gear, +1 variants of some older gear, and enemies which are randomly empowered with new abilities. - General QOL is pretty good. - Some achievements are an absolute slog. This didn't factor into my review, but if you like 100%ing games, you should be prepared for a boring grind at the end.

Questo gioco mi ispirava da prima della sua uscita e, tutto sommato, mi ha piacevolmente sorpreso più del previsto, anche in base ad alcune recensioni negative lette. E' forse vero che alle volte, a seconda dell'arma utilizzata, il combattimento si limiti allo svolgimento delle medesime mosse, ma non ritengo sia cosa da criticare tanto negativamente, ché in fondo non sono pochi i capolavori del genere che vedono il continuo reiterare delle stesse azioni. Il gioco è molto piacevole, sia nelle più immediate percezioni (lo stile grafico è accattivante, nei suoi giochi di luci, ombre e colori), sia nella crescita del personaggio che negli equipaggiamenti (non i più vari nei generi d'ispirazione, ma neanche miseri). Insomma, a me il gioco sta piacendo, e lo consiglio tranquillamente, a prescindere dalla passione per i metroidvania o i souls.

Хорошая метройдвания с множеством скрытых локаций квестов и возвращений назад за лутом. 本作的美术在所有2D平台游戏中可以说是无出其右，虽然艺术性比奥日系列略显逊色，但是本作用庞大的体量、制作精细且跨度大复用率极低的场景、以及3D游戏级别的光照与贴图，让所有同类游戏都相形见绌，只此一家，哪怕只是跑图看风景也是一种享受。另外，本作的角色动画，特效，反馈也十分顶级，几乎做到了无可挑剔。  本作的另一个闪光点是媲美类暗黑游戏的技能树和build构建。本作一共有6个技能树，每个技能树都错综复杂，也可以通过多种方式连接到其他系的技能树，达到多系混搭的效果。技能树中的很多perk可以极大地改变游戏玩法，例如我喜欢当血牛莽着近战打，就可以点出火系的“复活”“所有伤害改为DOT伤害，攻击敌人可以减少这些待定的伤害”来增加容错，以及光明系的一些特定情况下获得护盾的一系列perk来撑起更高的血量。另外，六系中每系都有至少五到六个主动技能，每个主动技能又有各自的升级路线，在技能搭配上也有很高的自由度。无论玩家偏好哪种风格的玩法，都可以在本作中找到适合的流派，组出一套适合自己的build。  本作的地图设计尚可，地形丰富，编排合理，但也没有一些让人觉得精妙的设计。剧情是本作的薄弱项，世界观有展开但是并不吸引人，走向七平八稳，演出表现力同样一般。但如果只是作为一个驱动玩家的目标来说，也足够了。  本作的矛盾点在于，在某些方面打磨的非常精细，而在另外一些方面又很粗糙，充满赶工痕迹。例如，本作的小怪种类数量尚可，大致有七十多种，虽然有些换皮，但是也基本都有独特的能力，在探图时也能感受到怪物的多样性。然而，本作却有大量一模一样的小boss反复出现，一种小boss经常要打四五遍，经常是打了一遍又来一遍，让人厌烦。另外，游戏中一些跑酷部分做的也很糟糕，缺乏打磨，比如非常容易失误的钩爪，几乎不存在的土狼时间，设计问题导致玩家不清楚下方是否有平台导致摔落等等。一些问题在游戏后续更新时已经得到了修复，例如即死陷阱改成了直接让玩家重生在附近区域，但发售以后的仓促改动更加说明了这些部分的欠打磨。  本作还有一个令人感到十分不解的点，就是大量的资源收集内容，说白了就是“得肝”，否则无法制作出强力装备，也无法解锁一些被动增益。而很多早期的资源在后期依然会需要，又很难找到高效率的获取方式，很容易在此浪费大量时间，令人恼火。  十分推荐给喜欢画面的玩家，以及喜欢丰富build构建的玩家游玩； 不推荐给希望体验到紧凑设计，接受不了重复内容的玩家，或是对剧情有要求的玩家。   [h1][b]8/10[/b][/h1]

TL;DR: Wish we had the option for "neutral" vote. First I gave this game a negative review (as a completionist) but changed to positive because some people might enjoy the game for what it is and don't care about 100%. From a completionist perspective the game becomes tedious to "farm" some of the latest achievements. From a casual point of view the game is gorgeous. A bit too pricey for my liking so, if you can, get it on sale.  The Positive:   - Graphics are absolutely gorgeous. The art direction is really good. - The OST is superb. - The voice acting is, overall, great. Especially for some NPC's. - Animations and movement are really well done (skills, crouching, sprinting, idle stance, cinematics, etc) - Leveling up and Talent Tree. You have 6 different Talent Trees. You choose one at the beginning and then, at level 25, the rest of the talent trees become available to you. I went hybrid using dual daggers and greatsword with the chance to apply poison, bleed and weakness. And dealing more damage the more adrenaline I get. Pretty fun! - The story is not great but it keeps you hooked 'till the end. - The Bestiary is gorgeous. Just like classic metroidvanias the more you kill one specific type of monster, the more you know about it (weaknesses, resistances, loot that drops and some lore) - Enchanting your gear. Runes to boost your stats, elemental, raw damage, etc.  The Negative:  - Even thoug you have tons to explore, the map, sometimes feels "empty". Making it a bit tedious to explore. - Bosses and elites/mini bosses are way too repetitive. Attack patterns feel the same albeit some new attack as you progress and they become a bit more difficult. Wraiths, rats, giants, fire demons are all the same skin and patterns (actually the giants have different skins depending on the biome but the attacks are exactly the same). - The Rift/Void feels the same every time you enter. It's just a platformer devoid of design and some rift monsters for you to kill before the time runs out. Yeah, there's a timer. Which makes sense. The Rift is a dangerous place lore wise but I expected more. - The grind becomes boring and tedious IMO. I love grinding in rouge-like, metroidvanias SP games but if you want to get all achievements in Mandragora be ready to grind A LOT. I spent 69 hours and I'm sitting at 79/85 achievements. One of them is to kill 10k monsters. I'm at 7k and some change. I'm missing 1 OFFHAND weapon to get the achievement to get all weapons... Don't know where it is. I'll check a guide for it. Anyways my point being: GOOD LUCK grinding for ALL THE MATERIALS you need to craft said weapons. Copper Ore to make them ingots, then bronze, silver, gold, steel, mythril and adamantine... Not to mention different jewels and leather you need. And I was using items like the TREASURE SEEKER and CUTPURSE CAPE (which augments the item drop rate from enemies and objects like boxes). Also you can loot ore from different ore veins BUT once depleted you have to wait for it to REPLENISH. Which can take several minutes. Why? Why wait on a single player game. Especially when you have to GRIND a lot. I will try to get all achievements but to kill 10k monsters and I'm already at 70 hours with 7k... Feels more like a chore and not enjoyment. - There's an achievement to enchant 50 PIECES OF ARMOR. Who wants to do that? Most enchantments gives you +4 to any stat or weight reduce, maybe some health and mana. You should enchant your main gear. Maybe 2 sets if you want to try things out. But 50 pieces of armor?! It seems devs wanted to "fill" the achievement table with something so they went crazy with... Wait for it... GRINDING.  - The achievements. Some are good and fun to get but the later ones are just tedious. Get every weapon (grind like crazy for materials because you need to craft most of them), KILL 10K MONSTERS (why?), enchant 50 armor pieces (who is even going to use 50 different pieces?). Make achievements fun to grind for or to offer some challenge (kill a boss without taking damage or kill 3 bosses without healing. Maybe even kill a boss with a fire build and another with poison. I don't know but make it fun).   CONCLUSION:  Overall the game is fun and really good in terms of character progression, learning new skills and trying different builds. And the art direction is superb. The atmosphere is gloomy, dark, gritty and warm when it needs to be. Some NPC's, quests and certain areas are gorgeous and polished. But the grinding and the Rift being so devoid of monsters and ambience to see some floating platforms for you to jump and progress is so boring. And if you're looking to get all achievements the grinding becomes TEDIOUS to say the least. Also fighting elites feels the same every single time. No variety there. I got the game during sale and I would suggest people to do the same. Because this game at full price with the amount of grind and repetitive tasks it has... Not worth it. At least for me (maybe Silksong ruined metroidvanias for me XD).

I really tried to give this game a fair shake, but I can't help but feel that it's just overwhelmingly mediocre in nearly all respects. Its best components have caveats, and anything else is either unremarkable or subpar.   The TL;DR is that if you're in the mood for this EXACT type of game (2D action RPG with modest Dark Souls and Metroidvania influences) then maybe, MAYBE this will scratch your itch. But, given the price point and overall lack of wow factor, I really hesitate to recommend it at all. Is Mandragora a bad game? Possibly not. But I don't really think it's worth your money, and I'm not entirely sure it's worth your time. Read on for specifics, but that's the gist of what you need to know.  /// RPG Progression ///   I'll start here because this is where I think the game's highest highs are. Credit where credit is due, I like a good skill tree, and Madragora has skill trees in spades. Each (actually cool) starting class has a relatively expansive skill tree associated with it, generally sporting cool passives and interesting effects. What's more is that you can actually multiclass at higher levels, which breaks character customization wide open, and skill trees "next" to each other in the game's UI have some light synergies and shortcuts.   But there's a downside to the skill tree, and that's that it also contains your stats. Spending talent points on nodes connecting class-related talents is how you add to base stats (Strength, Endurance, etcetera). And I'm going to be honest, I don't like this. The typical Soulslike level system offers far greater flexibility, in my opinion; Mandragora borrows a lot from Dark Souls, but strangely not that. Leveling up in Mandragora grants one talent point and nothing else, and the stats available to you are thusly determined by the nodes you have access to, and influenced by the nodes you're headed toward. Maybe you PoE fans out there will like the "routing" aspect of this leveling tree system, but I don't. I wish the leveling system for stats was typical fare, and talent points (for the actual cool stuff) were handled differently.  /// Aesthetics and atmosphere ///   This is probably the most subjective (and possibly the most controversial) of my takes, but I think Mandragora's art direction and overall mood are terrible. The game is clearly interested in having a somewhat dark, serious feel, and a decent hand-drawn opening scene with good voice acting sets this up well. But the actual art style is honestly horrid. It's this silly, cartoony style that's completely incongruous with the tone Mandragora is trying to achieve. It's almost World of Warcraft-esque, complete with goofy hoods and dorky proportions. That works for a casual-audience MMO; I don't think it works for a game trying to cultivate witchy Dark Souls vibes. And the immersion is further ruined because every human enemy spouts off the F-bomb every other line; I don't mind swearing, but it's giving "written by a 12 year old" and I'm not here for it. Absolute whiff on the aesthetics, and it is a considerable reason why I don't really like the game.  Oh, and I forgot the music. It's forgettable!  /// Combat ///  Combat is the other side of the RPG coin, and Mandragora's is mixed-to-bad. I like the basic setup: you have your basic attack, two active skills, and your trusty dodge roll. You can eventually swap between another set of three skills. I think this is a system with solid depth; there's enough to play with, but not too much complexity such that it bogs you down.   Unfortunately, this is where my compliments end, because actually fighting enemies feels terrible. First off, hitboxes are atrocious. They're are bad in the sense that the hurtbox extends visually beyond the physical bounds of enemies, but also in the sense that it can be kind of hard to tell what is an attack and what is simply part of the enemy asset's animation. If something isn't holding a sword or shooting a projectile at you, it can be unclear when and how it will actually damage you. Is that tentacle wriggle an attack, or just part of its idle animation? Good luck telling!  This is compounded by absolutely terrible sound effects -- quite possible the weakest I've ever heard in a game. They are so bad that I can barely tell they exist, and that goes for both enemies and the player. There is virtually no audio feedback when you hit, or are hit by, an enemy. This can make it pretty difficult to tell when you take damage; I honestly can't often tell unless I actually look directly at my healthbar to check. And, on the player-side, weak SFX make hitting an enemy deeply unsatisfying. I swing my giant flaming mace, and it makes the world's softest "plink" that barely sounds over the ambient audio track. Booooo. Add on a general sense of floatiness, and combat is weightless despite the obvious and deliberate Dark Souls influence.  So yeah, combat actually kinda sucks now that I really think about it. There's also all the standard annoying Souls stuff like ranged enemies placed up on platforms. My time spent in Nioh 2 has numbed me to this, but if you don't like that, better get over it.  /// World Layout and Exploration ///  I think it's fine. Zones start VERY small (like, literally 4-6 screens), but the game does open up more after the first hour or two. Once you get to the Dilapidated Mausoleum, you can start to tell that the game designers have actually played a Metroidvania. On that note, upgrades are slow (I haven't gotten a single one yet), but this is more of a 2D adventure game than a "true" Metroidvania. There's a lot of "cheating" to the interconnectivity anyhow, like background door objects that just kinda teleport you to an adjacent zone, rather than actually connecting with a tunnel or via negative space in the game.   It's all fine, though... absolutely NOTHING compared to a wonderful game like Blasphemous, but it'll do. I think Mandragora's exploration is really hurt by its poor aesthetics. If this game had good art and music, I think it would be muuuch more enjoyable to uncover the world. But instead we have a horde of swearing cartoon bandits, so it's just not that fun to explore. Maybe if you dislike the aesthetics less than I do, you'll like exploring more.   /// Crafting and itemization ///  Dear loooord the crafting system in this game is so banal and uninteresting. It might as well not exist. Just the same uninteresting cookie cutter "gather 2-3 items of this tier and bring to the merchant" that we've seen a million times before. It's so deeply unremarkable that I think it actually takes more away from the game than it adds. Crafting blueprints are also the primary reward for exploring tough optional paths, so the boring crafting system hurts exploration, too.   Thankfully, some enemies drop equippable items directly... but the game really wants you to craft, and I just really don't want to. I will admit I am a Crafting Hater (as anyone with taste is), but it's particularly boring in Mandragora. Just a constant sea of reagent pickups that I could not care less about. The items themselves are fine, go for the primary stats you want and then balance your equip load. The rings give +% numbers to slightly more desirable stats, whoopee. It's inoffensive but bland.   /// Conclusion ///  I kind of put my conclusion at the beginning of this review, wasn't that nice of me? I saved you a lot of time scrolling all this way. You can go back up to the top and read it again if you'd like. But basically, this game just isn't very good. There's a sense of effort and budget behind it, but nothing about it is... actually that fun?   I'm not sure what went wrong; I think Mandragora tries to do a lot of things moderately well, rather than trying to do a few things exceptionally well. Look at Nine Sols: god-tier masterpiece smartly constrained by a core vision (parry enemies, save surrogate son, that's about it). I just hit the Steam Review character limit. Skip Mandragora!

This game is a really solid souls like/metroidvania. It isn't overly punishing though you can adjust the difficulty up or down if you want to fine tune it more. It has some rough edges with the platforming but overall you aren't required to do much intensive platforming. Finding upgrades to access new areas is satisfying.   For completionists the game does give you a 100% checkmark for areas so you can track down where you might have miss items. On top of this you can also place markers to go back to areas later if needed. The build system is fun and there are a ton of build variety potential with the passive trees between the different classes and being able to multi class.   There is a bit of grinding for upgrading your NPC's and I wasn't much of a fan of this aspect. You will need to repeatedly grind materials from mobs to fully upgrade everything. Hopefully they lessen this in a future patch. Having the ability salvage items would very easily solve this issue.  Overall a very solid 8/10 game that I would easily recommend fans of souls likes and metroidvanias. Hopefully the devs get enough support to keep updating the game and maybe DLC or a sequel in the future.

I held off on this game for some time because the reviews seemed mixed.  That was a mistake.  This game is wonderful.  I'm sure it's not for everyone, but then again no game is.  This one though?  It was made for me.  First off the game is beautiful.  I am in love with the various environments.  The colors and lighting look amazing, both on my PC and steam deck.  Admittedly, the game really shines in 4k. The combat is souls-lite, in that you have a stamina bar so you can't swing wildly, you have dodge roll and can parry with a shield, or you can wield spells.  There are several classes to choose from at the start, but you do eventually get to build a multiclass thing.. I'm not there yet.  I find the combat really good.  Some of the bosses have been tough... I have had to try a couple times, and that's only a few hours into the game.  You do have a corpse run, but I've found that the save shrines are reasonably well put.  You never really feel too, too far from one.  A bit further sometimes that I would like but not so bad that it ever really bothered me.  Close enough to boss rooms that you may have a few things to swing at on your way back, but nothing too difficult. This is first and foremost a combat game.  Very little actual platforming, and certainly no advanced traversal.  This is a side scrolling combat rpg.   And that brings me to where this game really shines for me... this isn't your average Metroidvania, this is a full fledged RPG.  Everything from side quests, bounty board, and idle chat in the towns... it feels like a full on mainstream RPG, but just from the side.  I love that.  The story is engrossing and dripping with theme, and some of the environments are just fantastic.  As I said above, this is souls-lite, not souls-like... this isn't Grime (which I love) or Salt & Sanctuary (which I loathe).  It's definitely meant for you to not button mash, but as long as you can control your urges then this game isn't so hard that you cannot progress.  As you level up you build out your little camp by the Witch Tree and get access to vendors, crafting items, etc.  All fairly standard fare... for an rpg.  First time I've seen it this deep though in a Metroidvania.  And make no mistake... this is a Metroidvania in the sense that you have a full Metroidvania map, there are ability and item gated pathways you will need to backtrack to, etc.  Fast travel is from shrine to shrine.  Anyway I am so glad I've finally tried this one out... this is a good example of why sometimes I need to stop paying such close attention to steam reviews.  I'm absolutely loving this game.  It's the right level of challenge for me and has all of the theme, color, story, and ambiance that I really need to have in a game.  Looking forward to more of it!

You know the meme of the horse drawing where the first half is on fire and good looking and the second half is like a child's drawing? This is the game equivalent of that meme  Let me explain: it starts strong, the visuals draw you in, the combat feels balanced, you're challenged but it's fair, the story is nothing amazing but it's sesrviceable. This is how it went for me in the first, I'd say 15 hours?   But, boy oh boy, does ♥♥♥♥ hits the fan. I see a lot of reviews having a hard time saying what type of game this is and to me, this is a metroidvania on the platforming side and a souls-like in the fighting side. The thing is, you can make both of these things feels solid in the first few hours of any game. The second half is pretty much where it can fall apart and fall apart is does. Metroidvania means you'll get movement skills and that's where it becomes hard to balance with the souls-like aspect: you don't want the player to just cheese the fight by double/triple/fly jump over the boss... but you just can do that passed the second half of the game... So how is it fixed? More enemies thrown at you and they just become damage sponge  And that? That's just ONE problem so far. I'll make short of the others but basically: - Level design is good in the first half, TERRIBLE on the second half... So many hazardous death from platforming and I dunno why, but the devs thought it was a neat idea to but spike traps/holes every-♥♥♥♥♥♥♥-where... It's not challeging, it's just bad desing, full stop - The story started very meh but gives you enough to get you going and then it just doesn't matter... Seriously, you can romance another character and you speak with her like 4 times tops. It's just there to be there, it's bad. You stop receiving messages from your "boss" about your mission about halfway like it doesn't even matter anymore... - Art style is sublime, it's an acquired taste and unsettling and that's personal but it at least has an identity on that front - My biggest gripe: Level design... Bad platforming, alternate reality section that just straight up sucks and then they get worse EVERY. SINGLE. TIME. cause apparently, you need new mechanics to keep the player engage, which is goes to my next point... - This game has more padding than a pillow factory and it's tied directly with the poor level design. Lots of empty spaces...  AND...  As I'm writing this review, the trailer for the 1.6 update is playing and I saw "Witch Stone near the final boss" and all the "balance" they've made and it makes me even more furious because it's still janky, unbalanced and bad overall.  I'mma be honest, I ragequitted at the last section of the game cause it's just not fun anymore. The last boss fight I did? I put a Damage Over Time effect on the ground and cheese the boss with movement skills. That was when I snapped and figured I'm better off not finishing this cause the story won't save it  Very very dissapointed, especially at that price point and seeing it was Kickstarted and this is update 1.6 makes me feel the game was underbaked and needed at least 1 or 2 years more in the oven (There's a Kickstarter donation hall that you need to read ALL the names to unlock the treasure... oh and there's a spike pit thrown in there too cause you know, level design is GREAT)  I'm legit happier writing this review than finishing up this game. Feels like I'm expelling all the rage from the bad level design and now I can pick my next game to play

The plot is your standard "witchcraft good, religion bad' thing that has become a standardized cliche at this point, and the graphics are ok, ish.   Ultimately, there's no reason at all to play this when other games in the genre exist that do everything this does but at a much higher and much more enjoyable level.
